source_jobs.title: Developer Relations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI



How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?




Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We're a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.




We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.





THE ROLE



Fractional AI is widely regarded by practitioners as one of the best teams at AI implementation in complex scenarios. Our brand awareness among top-tier engineers hasn't kept pace with our output. On May 20, we became the founding team of Anthropic's new AI Services Business, and it's the right moment to bring in our first Developer Relations Engineer to build a lasting engineering brand.




You'll be embedded in the engineering team, reading code, sitting in architecture reviews, talking to clients , and translating that work into content, community, and presence that engineers actually respect.





WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Write and publish technical content — blog posts, project teardowns, HN writeups — drawn from what the team is actually building

 - Build Fractional's presence on channels where strong engineers spend time: Hacker News, technical podcasts, conferences, and niche communities

 - Run high-signal community touchpoints: dinners, workshops, small events where top engineers encounter Fractional in a non-transactional context

 - Drive open source contributions and external projects that reflect Fractional's engineering caliber

 - Build partnerships with complementary technical brands — co-sponsoring events, co-publishing content, cross-pollinating communities

 - Feed signal back to engineering leadership: what practitioners are excited about, skeptical of, building toward
   
   
   


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Strong engineering foundation. You've shipped software and cared about doing it right. You understand why good system design matters and can have a credible technical conversation with a senior engineer about AI architecture, eval design, or production tradeoffs.

 - Exceptional writing. You write with clarity and specificity. You know the difference between content that earns attention and content that fills a feed. You've written things engineers have actually read and shared.

 - Taste for what resonates technically. You have a feel for the developer zeitgeist — what practitioners find credible, what reads as hype, what makes an HN post land versus get flagged.

 - High ownership mindset. You jump in without instruction, embrace a "no job too big, no job too small" mindset, and want to shape strategy and culture from day one.

 - Low ego, high integrity. You help others and ask for help. You hold a high bar for honesty, with yourself and your team.
   
   
   


ENGINEERING AT FRACTIONAL



We're opinionated about what works in production, and we write about it — from why most eval setups fail by collapsing everything into a single score https://engineering.fractional.ai/your-evals-have-a-rotten-tomatoes-problem, to why defaulting to chat interfaces limits AI's impact https://engineering.fractional.ai/stop-designing-chatbots.



We have a distinct approach to building AI systems: applying standard software engineering discipline to the non-deterministic world of frontier AI. We design systems around testable hypotheses, curate durable datasets, and ensure what we ship keeps working long after we've handed it over.



After shipping 30+ AI products, we have strong opinions on what makes AI products succeed and how to get them into production. We think you'll find it refreshing if you've seen how most companie

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer (SF/NY)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI

How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?

Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We’re a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.

We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.

We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh-Durham, and Dubai.




THE ROLE

This role is designed for former engineering leaders (IC or EM) or founders who are comfortable owning end-to-end technical outcomes but specifically want to continue being impactful as individual contributors and spend more time in the code.



You'll work on a small, high-caliber team (2–5 engineers and a PM) building AI products for our clients. You'll set technical direction, write code, and be the person the team looks to when something is hard.



You'll spend roughly 75% of your time in code and 25% working directly with clients — often their CTOs — understanding problems, walking through tradeoffs, and making sure what we’re building meets their needs.



Most engineers take a decade to see this range of hard problems across this many domains. Here, you'll do it in your first year. To make this possible, we are religious about being the best company in the world to learn Applied AI engineering practices.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 8+ years of engineering experience, with deep care for the craft. You've shipped multiple complete products end-to-end and write elegant, production-ready code across multiple disciplines.

 - Specific interest in applying software engineering fundamentals to AI systems. You care about balancing frontier model capabilities with good system design.

 - Comfort talking with senior technical stakeholders. You navigate conversations skillfully, and care about people using what you build.

 - High ownership mindset. You jump in without instruction, embrace a "no job too big, no job too small" mindset, and want to shape strategy and culture.

 - Low ego, high integrity. You help others and ask for help. You hold a high bar for honesty–with yourself and your team.
   
   


OUR ENGINEERING PHILOSOPHY

We're opinionated about what works in production, and we write about it — from why most eval setups fail https://engineering.fractional.ai/your-evals-have-a-rotten-tomatoes-problem by collapsing everything into a single score, to why defaulting to chat interfaces https://engineering.fractional.ai/stop-designing-chatbots limits AI's impact.



We have a distinct approach to building AI systems: applying standard software engineering discipline to the non-deterministic world of frontier AI. We design systems around testable hypotheses, curate durable data sets, and ensure what we ship keeps working long after we've handed it over.



After shipping 30+ AI products, we have strong opinions on what makes AI products succeed and how to get them into production. We think you'll find it refreshing if you've seen how most companies approach AI.




HOW FRACTIONAL IS DIFFERENT

A typical engineer here ships two to three products per year and learns from dozens more. You have outsized autonomy on each product — but you won't have a year to polish a single system. The upside is constant time on the frontier of models and tooling, and a muscle for AI product development that's hard to build anywhere else.



Most engineers look back at this as the biggest growth period of their career. But it's possible you won't like it — we recommend using the interview process to hear what our engineers think.




OUR VALUES

 - Overdeliver: We are writing the playbo

source_jobs.title: Talent Partner - Engineering
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI

How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?

Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We’re a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.

We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.

We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh-Durham, and Dubai.




THE ROLE

Our talent is our product. Customers choose Fractional AI because we bring together elite engineers, product leaders, and commercial operators to build and deploy applied AI systems.



We’re building a team that makes Fractional the best place to learn applied AI. As a member of our talent team, you’ll play a critical role in making that real — identifying and closing exceptional talent. Engineering hiring is core to the role, but you’ll have the opportunity to work across functions as we grow.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Source exceptional talent: You’ll identify and engage top candidates, with a primary focus on engineering hiring.

 - Own the candidate experience end-to-end: From first touch through close, you’ll build trust, understand motivations, and ensure a high-signal, high-quality process for every candidate.

 - Build on a high-performing recruiting process: You’ll come alongside a strong existing system and help evolve it — bringing new ideas and using data, AI, and experimentation to refine how we evaluate and close talent.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Track record of hiring top talent: You’ve successfully hired founding engineers or staff/principal engineers in early-stage or high-growth environments.

 - Strong judgment and empathy: You know what great looks like and build trust quickly with candidates and hiring teams.

 - Data-driven and experimental mindset: You use data and experimentation to improve how you source, evaluate, and close candidates.

 - Technical fluency: You understand modern engineering stacks and can credibly assess how candidates map to team needs.

 - High ownership with a bias for leverage: You operate with precision and follow-through, and look for ways to scale your impact through tooling, automation, and AI.




OUR VALUES

 - Overdeliver: We are writing the playbook on how to create enterprise value with LLMs, and building our reputation as the world’s best applied AI team.

 - Overuse AI: We go out of our way to experiment with the latest tools and share what we learn with our teammates - building a culture at the frontier.

 - Over "engineer" the culture: For every big decision, we ask “would this make Fractional a better or worse place for our team?”
   
    


LOCATION

San Francisco, CA
New York, NY

Work Arrangement: 4 days in-person, Fridays remote

 


BENEFITS

 - 🏦 Competitive Compensation Package: Attractive salary aligned with your experience and skills.

 - 📈 401k with Matching: Invest in your future with our company-matched retirement plan.

 - 🌴 Unlimited PTO: Take the time you need to relax and rejuvenate.

 - 🏥 Exceptional Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage: Comprehensive health benefits to keep you and your family healthy.

 - 🚑 Health FSA: Manage out-of-pocket health expenses effectively.

 - 🚌 Commuter Benefits: Make your travel to work easier and more affordable.

 - 🏥 Life Insurance: Providing peace of mind for you and your loved ones.

 - 🎉 Team Events and Activities: Regular events to build camaraderie and a strong team spirit.

 


READY TO JOIN US?

source_jobs.title: Bring Your Own Engineering Team
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI

How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?

Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We’re a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.

We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.

We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh-Durham, and Dubai.




ABOUT BRINGING YOUR OWN ENGINEERING TEAM

Some teams are just too good to break up. Maybe you co-founded a venture that's pivoting, maybe your team got caught in a reduction in force, or maybe you're looking for the next thing to build together. We already work in small pods: tight-knit teams of engineers working on a single project together. Joining Fractional is a natural way to keep your team intact and shift your focus to building for clients with instant product-market fit. If you're part of an existing team of 2–5 engineers, we encourage you to apply to join Fractional AI together.




HOW IT WORKS

 - One interview process. We'll arrange your interviews on the same day, with the same panel, against the same bar we hold every Fractional engineer to.

 - One offer. If we decide to move forward, we'll make offers to your whole team at the same time. You're each free to accept or decline individually — though we hope you'll all say yes.

 - One first project. We'll work to staff you on an engagement together.




BYOT IN PRACTICE: FABIUS

In 2026, we were introduced to the Fabius team — a Y Combinator (W23) startup that had spent three years building AI to automate sales workflows. They had a track record of building complex AI systems, and they'd spent considerable time perfecting the craft of partnering closely with customers to make sure their solutions delivered results. The more we talked, the more we saw a team whose strengths mapped almost exactly to what we hire for. For Fabius, joining Fractional was a natural way to focus on what they were best at — building AI-powered products — while staying together as a team.



"I was surprised how much autonomy Fractional expected us to keep. Suddenly we had very interested, very motivated clients — and the same hard technical problems we used to grind through at Fabius. " — Neil Madsen, Fabius Co-founder



"When we spoke with Eddie and Chris, it felt like a bunch of founders hanging out. There was a certain expectation that the team itself was an asset, and Fractional was specifically interested in protecting the way we were working together, rather than figuring out how to make everyone play a narrow role." - Andy Day, Fabius Co-founder




WHO WE HIRE

We generally expect your team to fall into the categories of one of our existing engineering and PM roles — most commonly Software Engineer, Senior/Staff Software Engineer or Forward Deployed PM. Take a look at those JDs to see what we look for at the individual level. We evaluate teams holistically on top of that, but we expect every team member to clear a similar bar as all other Fractional team members.

 - Software Engineer https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/fractional-ai/21e0ffe5-1f24-4417-b2a8-507846c1bc38

 - Senior / Staff Software Engineer https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/fractional-ai/22e6bcd3-b80d-462d-bd79-9deae131ee94

 - Forward Deployed Product Manager https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/fractional-ai/d93607e0-3e38-4a6f-a05e-f293d3f3bdf4
   
   


OUR ENGINEERING PHILOSOPHY

We're opinionated about what works in production, and we write about it — from why most eval setups fail by collapsing everything into a single score, to why defaulting to chat interfaces limits AI's impact.

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI

How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?

Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We’re a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.

We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.

We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh-Durham, and Dubai.

The Role
You’ll be the operational backbone supporting our CEO and CTO directly, and developing a deep understanding of their priorities, working styles, and what good looks like for each of them. It’s a role where your judgment, taste, and systems thinking show up every day.

 

 - Part calendar owner — you develop a real point of view on how their time should be spent and help them protect it.

 - Part systems builder — you design the workflows and rhythms that keep executives organized and aligned.

 - Part communications operator — you communicate effectively, coordinate what needs to move, and make sure the right things get attention at the right time.

 - Part operator — you proactively spot the gaps and keep things moving.

 

If you treat upward management as a craft and get genuine energy from making great leaders even more effective, this role is for you.

What You'll Do

Own the calendar with a point of view: Manage complex, high-volume scheduling for our CEO and CTO. You’ll develop a point of view on their priorities and use that context to make smart calls.

Keep them ahead of what’s coming: Track open commitments, surface blockers, and make sure executives are informed, prepared, and ready for what’s next.

Build systems that create leverage: Design and maintain the intake processes, communication rhythms, and workflows that keep the executive team organized and aligned.

Be the communications layer: Draft, triage, and coordinate communications on behalf of the CEO and CTO. Synthesize inbound into clear recommended actions.

Make the logistics invisible: Plan and execute complex travel, meetings, team gatherings, and offsites with a high bar for experience and detail.

Be a calm, trusted presence: Act as a steady, positive and trusted point of contact across internal teams, external clients and partners, and stakeholders.

What You Bring

Executive support experience in a high-growth environment: You’ve supported senior leaders through fast-moving, high-stakes periods. We typically look for 5–8 years, but we care more about your prior roles and impact than years on a resume.

Upward management as a craft: You know how to keep busy leaders organized, informed, and accountable. You anticipate what they need, flag what they haven’t seen yet, and take pride in making them more effective.

Strong written communication: Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to write clearly and naturally in someone else’s voice when needed.

A bias for systems and leverage: You’re always looking for ways to make the operation run more smoothly — through better systems, processes, and tooling.

Sharp prioritization instincts: You anticipate conflicts, make tradeoffs independently, and surface what needs attention. You can hold a lot of context at once and keep the rhythm going without creating overhead.

Excitement about AI tools: You’re excited about how AI can create leverage in your own workflow.

Presence, composure, and discretion: People trust you quickly because you’re calm under pressure, thoughtful in how you engage, and consistent in how you show up

Our Values

 - Overdeliver: We are writing the playbook on how to create enterprise value with LLMs, and bu

source_jobs.title: Corporate Development
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI



How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?



Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We're a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.



On May 20, we became the founding team behind a new AI services business backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.




THE ROLE

We're a CEO's first call when they're thinking about AI transformation. To deliver on that promise at scale, we need to deepen our bench, growing our core offering and building new capabilities that let us offer an end-to-end solution across the AI transformation lifecycle.



A meaningful portion of our growth will be inorganic: acquihires to rapidly scale our team of frontier AI builders, and strategic acquisitions to expand into adjacent service lines. This role exists to own that motion — from mapping the landscape, to closing deals, and driving successful integration.



You'll work directly with our CEO to identify, structure, and execute these opportunities. This is a senior, high-ownership role with direct exposure to the most consequential decisions we make as a company.




WHAT YOU'LL DO



 - Strategic partnerships. Identify collaboration opportunities (e.g., joint go-to-market motions and delivery frameworks) with other players in the ecosystem who have complementary capabilities.

 - Service line expansion. Define how we expand across the AI transformation lifecycle. Identify and prioritize where to build, partner, or acquire and build the investment thesis for each.

 - Deal sourcing & origination. Build and maintain a proprietary target universe across acquihires, tuck-ins, and capability acquisitions. Actively cultivate relationships with founders, investors, and advisors so the best deals come to us before they go to market.

 - Transaction execution. Own the full deal lifecycle: financial modeling, LOI negotiation, diligence coordination, and close. Structure creative deal types (earnouts, minority stakes, partnerships with options) that create alignment without overpaying.

 - Integration & value realization. Lead post-close integration from day one of diligence. Define success metrics, map ownership, and set 90-day milestones before a deal closes.
   
   


WHAT YOU BRING



 - You've operated at the intersection of commercial and technical — as a founder, investor, or operator — and have the instincts to show for it. You know how to build relationships that turn into revenue, and how to close.

 - You've led the full M&A lifecycle, from sourcing through integration, and know what separates a deal that creates real capability from one that looks good on paper.

 - You bring strong financial modeling skills and fluency in deal structuring and valuation — LBO/DCF analysis, precedent transactions, trading comps.

 - You have sharp judgment on the AI landscape. You know what's real and what's hype, where the white space is, and how the pieces fit together across the enterprise transformation stack.

 - You build trust quickly with senior stakeholders and are as comfortable in a boardroom as you are rolling up your sleeves to get a deal across the line.

 - High agency, low ego. You operate comfortably in ambiguity, with a bias toward action.




BENEFITS



🏦 Competitive Compensation Package

📈 401k with Matching

🌴 Unlimited PTO

🏥 Exceptional Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage

🚑 Health FSA

🚌 Commuter Benefits

🏥 Life Insurance

🎉 Team Events and Activities

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Product Manager (Dubai)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI

How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?

Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We’re a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.

We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.

We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh-Durham, and Dubai.




THE ROLE

Our customers come to us with ambitious, often fuzzy goals — make content moderation faster, customer service cheaper and better, unlock new integrations, automate the workflows no one else can touch.

Your job is to turn those goals into AI-powered solutions that work in the real world, and to own the journey from first conversation to live system to real business impact.

This is a forward-deployed, full-stack customer role:

 - Part engagement manager — you're the primary face of Fractional to the client.

 - Part product manager — you decide what we build and why.

 - Part delivery/program manager — you run the execution motion end-to-end.

 - Part “do whatever it takes" operator — you jump into the gaps to keep things moving.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the customer relationship end-to-end. Act as the customer's day-to-day strategic partner and primary point of contact, building trust across all levels of the organization and being candid about tradeoffs.

 - Shape what we build and why. Translate ambiguous business problems into crisp problem statements, user journeys, and success metrics — and work with customers to define what "success" actually means.

 - Turn messy problems into shipped solutions. Translate ambiguous needs into clear requirements, PRDs, and plans for engineers. Shape workflows, edge cases, and evaluation criteria for AI-powered systems.

 - Drive execution across multiple AI products. Run the day-to-day rhythm — planning, check-ins, risk tracking — and coordinate between customers, engineers, designers, and Fractional teammates to keep work unblocked and on time.

 - Help create demand, not just fulfill it. Jump on early, messy conversations where the project is still a vague idea. Help founders and sales tease out real pain, sketch solution approaches live, and spot expansion opportunities.

 - Drive adoption and long-term impact. Work with client teams on rollout, enablement, and change management so the systems you build actually get used. Bring back insights that inform Fractional's playbooks and product direction.

 - Bring judgment and clarity. When requirements are fuzzy or customers change course, be the person in the room who can say: here's what we're trying to do, here's what we know, here's the plan.


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Customer-facing, high-stakes experience. You've led projects where customers were under pressure, stakeholders weren't aligned, and the path wasn't obvious. You're comfortable pushing back, reframing the problem, and holding the line on quality. We typically look for 4–8 years in product, consulting, or implementations, although we care more about what you've done than the label on your resume.

 - Real product and delivery experience. You've built and shipped products with engineers and designers across multiple cycles. You can run an execution rhythm — stand-ups, planning, tracking, retros — without turning it into process theater. -

 - Structured problem-solving and communication. You ask good questions, structure chaos, and turn ambiguity into a plan — moving from whiteboarding with execs to writing a crisp doc for engineers without losing the plot.

 - Bias for action and ownership. You roll up your sl

After shipping 30+ AI products, we have strong opinions on what makes AI products succeed and how to get them into production. We think you'll find it refreshing if you've seen how most companies approach AI.

 


HOW FRACTIONAL IS DIFFERENT

A typical engineer here ships two to three products per year and learns from dozens more. You have outsized autonomy on each product — but you won't have a year to polish a single system. The upside is constant time on the frontier of models and tooling, and a muscle for AI product development that's hard to build anywhere else.

 

Most engineers look back at this as the biggest growth period of their career. But it's possible you won't like it — we recommend using the interview process to hear what our engineers think.

 


OUR VALUES

 - Overdeliver: We are writing

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Product Manager (SF/NY)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI

How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?

Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We’re a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.

We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.

We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh-Durham, and Dubai.




THE ROLE

Our customers come to us with ambitious, often fuzzy goals — make content moderation faster, customer service cheaper and better, unlock new integrations, automate the workflows no one else can touch.

Your job is to turn those goals into AI-powered solutions that work in the real world, and to own the journey from first conversation to live system to real business impact.

This is a forward-deployed, full-stack customer role:

 - Part engagement manager — you're the primary face of Fractional to the client.

 - Part product manager — you decide what we build and why.

 - Part delivery/program manager — you run the execution motion end-to-end.

 - Part “do whatever it takes" operator — you jump into the gaps to keep things moving.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the customer relationship end-to-end. Act as the customer's day-to-day strategic partner and primary point of contact, building trust across all levels of the organization and being candid about tradeoffs.

 - Shape what we build and why. Translate ambiguous business problems into crisp problem statements, user journeys, and success metrics — and work with customers to define what "success" actually means.

 - Turn messy problems into shipped solutions. Translate ambiguous needs into clear requirements, PRDs, and plans for engineers. Shape workflows, edge cases, and evaluation criteria for AI-powered systems.

 - Drive execution across multiple AI products. Run the day-to-day rhythm — planning, check-ins, risk tracking — and coordinate between customers, engineers, designers, and Fractional teammates to keep work unblocked and on time.

 - Help create demand, not just fulfill it. Jump on early, messy conversations where the project is still a vague idea. Help founders and sales tease out real pain, sketch solution approaches live, and spot expansion opportunities.

 - Drive adoption and long-term impact. Work with client teams on rollout, enablement, and change management so the systems you build actually get used. Bring back insights that inform Fractional's playbooks and product direction.

 - Bring judgment and clarity. When requirements are fuzzy or customers change course, be the person in the room who can say: here's what we're trying to do, here's what we know, here's the plan.


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Customer-facing, high-stakes experience. You've led projects where customers were under pressure, stakeholders weren't aligned, and the path wasn't obvious. You're comfortable pushing back, reframing the problem, and holding the line on quality. We typically look for 4–8 years in product, consulting, or implementations, although we care more about what you've done than the label on your resume.

 - Real product and delivery experience. You've built and shipped products with engineers and designers across multiple cycles. You can run an execution rhythm — stand-ups, planning, tracking, retros — without turning it into process theater. -

 - Structured problem-solving and communication. You ask good questions, structure chaos, and turn ambiguity into a plan — moving from whiteboarding with execs to writing a crisp doc for engineers without losing the plot.

 - Bias for action and ownership. You roll up your sl

source_jobs.title: Principal, AI Transformation
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI

How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?

Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We’re a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.

We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.

We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh-Durham, and Dubai.




THE ROLE

We're the call a CEO makes when they're ready to lead their industry into the age of AI — and the Principal is who answers.



Think of this function as a fractional Chief AI Transformation Officer for our clients. The expectation is for you to become the CEO's right hand and trusted confidant — the primary executive counterpart who helps them move their company into the AI era from a position of strength. It's a role closer to a partner at a top firm than a typical sales or delivery seat.



You'll own the full arc. You win the business, embed with the CEO to find where AI changes the game, and mobilize a team of world-class PMs and engineers to deliver results — not slideware. Our Principals drive the company's revenue, and they earn it on insight and Applied AI expertise.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Win the business. Convert warm, high-intent executive relationships — sourced through our consortium and Anthropic — into signed engagements. Bring the judgment to qualify hard, the credibility to earn trust fast, and the finesse to negotiate and close.

 - Become the CEO's fractional Chief AI Transformation Officer. Embed with leadership as their trusted advisor on AI, diagnosing where the real opportunity is and shaping a path the business will actually act on.

 - Mobilize delivery. Stand up engagements quickly and keep them pointed at outcomes. We're more Palantir than McKinsey — you orchestrate the work that ships value, not decks that sit on a shelf.

 - Own and grow the relationship. Stay the executive counterpart well past the first project, deepening trust and expanding the partnership over time.


WHAT YOU BRING

 - You earn executive trust fast. CEOs and their leadership teams come to see you as the right hand they call first. You're crisp and compelling in the room and on the page, with any audience.

 - Relentless learning agility and unmatched curiosity. You have a track record of ramping quickly and becoming an expert across a wide range of technical and industry topics. You don't need to be an Applied AI expert on day one — you do need the curiosity and horsepower to become an expert within 90 days, engage technical leaders as a peer, and turn pattern-matching across many engagements into a compounding advantage.

 - Strategic and business acumen. You see where AI creates real enterprise value and translate it into a path a business will move on.

 - You're relationship-driven. You build durable, high-trust relationships and own them for the long term — through the engagement and well beyond.

 - You're a partner teams want. Engineers and PMs are genuinely glad to have you in the room. You bring senior judgment and help resolve blockers.

 - Revenue instinct. You recognize the right moments to advance and win business, and you have the commercial drive, urgency and finesse to close.
   
   


OUR VALUES

 - Overdeliver: We are writing the playbook on how to create enterprise value with LLMs, and building our reputation as the world’s best applied AI team.

 - Overuse AI: We go out of our way to experiment with the latest tools and share what we learn with our teammates - building a culture at the frontier.

 - Over "engineer" the culture: For every big

source_jobs.title: Associate Principal, AI Transformation
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FRACTIONAL AI

How do you turn a decades-old dataset into an industry-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?

Fractional AI is focused on putting frontier AI to work. We’re a group of veteran Silicon Valley builders who care deeply about getting complex AI systems built right, with strong conviction about what makes them succeed.

We're backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, General Atlantic, Leonard Green & Partners, Apollo Global Management, GIC, and Sequoia Capital.

We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh-Durham, and Dubai.




THE ROLE

The fastest way to learn how AI will reshape businesses is to go inside and help transform dozens of them. As an Associate Principal, you're the engine of our AI strategic advisory work — partnering with Principals to set companies' AI transformation agendas, and assessing value-creation opportunities and disruption risk across industries in diligence. In a given quarter you'll see inside several businesses and build pattern recognition that no other role in the market can offer.



This is the path to becoming the “fractional Chief AI Transformation Officer” our clients call first. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with Principals on live engagements, proposals, and relationship building, taking on more of the selling motion as you go. The role is not quota-carrying today, but it's built to grow into one.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Drive AI strategic advisory engagements end-to-end. You run point strategic engagements that set a company's transformation agenda or running diligence on an asset

 - Support live deals and the selling motion. Work alongside Principals on active pursuits — joining discovery calls, shaping proposals, helping manage relationships — and learn how we scope, price, and position our work.

 - Build your network. Through engagements and deal support, build durable relationships with executives and investors.

 - Be a high-utility player. As an early-stage company, the work extends beyond any one engagement — you'll jump into adjacent streams like partnership development, thought leadership, or internal tooling and process improvement.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - High ownership, comfort with ambiguity. Engagement windows are tight, and you drive the work forward without waiting to be told what to do.

 - Learning agility and analytical horsepower. You ramp quickly on an unfamiliar industry, identify what matters, and form a defensible point of view — turning pattern-matching across engagements into a compounding advantage.

 - Crisp communication, written and verbal. You translate technical AI concepts into business language that lands with executives and deal teams.

 - Relationship-building instinct. You build rapport with people and are energized, not drained, by client-facing work.

 - AI-native. You're genuinely interested in AI and have a working understanding of what generative AI can and can't do. You don't need to be an engineer, but you need to be credible in a room with one.

 - Low ego, high resourcefulness. You're both a doer and a strategist.




3-MONTH SNAPSHOT

 - You've contributed to several AI strategic advisory engagements alongside our Principals, progressing from supporting role to execution lead.

 - You're independently driving key takeaways, executive interviews, and deliverable creationn on active engagements.

 - You've joined live sales or account conversations with a Principal, and are starting to understand how we scope, price, and position our work.

 - You've identified at least one way to improve how we operate — process, tooling, templates — and started making it better.




OUR VALUES

 - Overdeliver: We are writing the playbook on how to create enterprise value with LLMs, and building our reputation as the world’s

notes: Highly technical roles, strong AI focus, backed by prominent investors
